caption: Simplest way to level up your Abs workout
author: Jordan Yeoh
transcript: When you do leg raises, you might feel a lot on your lower back. But when you do reverse crunches, more lower abs, lesser lower back. When you do your crunches like this, you'll get neck pain. But when you place your hand behind your neck and crunch just like this, you'll use fully abs. When you go all the way up, that's hip flexor and lower back. But when you open up your knees and crunch through all the way up, you use a lot of abs, lesser lower back.
title: Fix Your Ab Exercises for Better Results
one_liner: Simple form tweaks to target abs more effectively and reduce lower back strain.
key_points: Replace leg raises with reverse crunches to hit lower abs and spare the lower back
Support your neck with hands during crunches to eliminate neck pain and fully engage abs
Open your knees when crunching upward to maximize ab activation and minimize hip flexor/lower back involvement
